human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for commercial construction execution-state management, permit and occupancy boundaries, completion-evidence handling, functionality coverage, and runtime-specification-based posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest defines the commercial construction operations lane around advancing, holding, reworking, completing, and escalating work using permit, site, inspection, schedule, change, subcontractor, occupancy, and closeout evidence.
Authority sources cover permitting, inspection, occupancy, site safety, project records, progress reporting, quality-control reporting, and closeout rather than only one dimension.
Functionality map covers all required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and construction-and-field-services abilities with scenario coverage for every scenario in evaluation results.
Research summary maps workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, and required scenario families to this commercial construction lane.

review_notes: No functional or evidence blockers remain for this package draft.

All required scenario families and escalation families are represented, including permit and site-readiness gaps, inspection and closeout blockers, unsupported completion, licensed-trade boundary refusal, policy conflict escalation, and tenant adaptation.

notes: validated conditions met for evidence-backed commercial construction operations specialist spec pack.
Runtime remains specification-based and must be promoted to a retained implementation only through separate governance and commit history.
